text: The article explores how different factors of international educational integration affect national reforms of vocational and higher education. The integrative processes of educational integration are described on different levels (from legislative regulation to the structure of higher education institutions, procedures of accreditation and quality assurance of educational activities and the management of educational material). The competitiveness of the educational market in Russia is estimated together with the positive and negative factors of the introduction of each of the considered reform components. The impact of the admission of international students to Russian universities is also analyzed. The relevance of the research results for departments of international cooperation of educational schools aiming to synchronise the development of Russian educational schools with the creation of a single global educational space is stated. Recommendations for academic management for designing strategies and educational materials for modernising professional education in the context of realisation of the Bologna and Copenhagen processes are also provided.
explanation: Contains very generic, buzzword-heavy phrasing with no concrete data or novel insights, a pattern often seen in AI-generated text. The wording could also reflect human academic writing, so the conclusion is uncertain.
